label: Lumina System Core
description: System-administration domain covering Lumina OS internals: CTL, RBAC, domain physics, domain packs, system physics, policy gates, and operational monitoring.

glossary.definition: Causal Trace Ledger — the append-only, hash-chained record of all domain-session trace events and commitment records.
A CTL record that marks a durable system decision: domain-physics activation, role change, escalation, or night-cycle outcome.
A single LLM-driven turn record in the CTL, capturing input evidence, state delta, and the system-physics hash active at the time.
The machine-readable global invariant layer (cfg/system-physics.yaml / .json) that constrains every domain session regardless of which domain is active.
A domain-pack JSON file defining the module's topics, glossary, standing orders, escalation conditions, and artifacts.
A self-contained module bundle (cfg, prompts, domain-lib, systools, modules) that implants a domain into the Lumina runtime.
A role with authoring rights over one or more governed modules; highest ceiling for domain-scoped operations.
The cross-domain governance layer above a Domain Authority; approves cross-domain synthesis and standing-order conflicts.
A hard invariant in system physics that cannot be overridden by any domain pack; violated by a session → escalation.
The linked sequence of record_hash values in the CTL that provides tamper-evidence across all ledger entries.
Role-Based Access Control — Lumina's permission model combining chmod-style octal modes with optional extended ACLs.
cfg/domain-registry.yaml — the file that maps domain_id keys to their runtime-config paths and routing metadata.
A persistent instruction authored by a Domain Authority that survives across session turns until explicitly revoked.
A domain-session signal that exceeds a policy-gate threshold; surfaces to Meta Authority and writes an escalation record to the CTL.
A configurable off-hours batch pass that performs glossary expansion, constraint refresh, and knowledge-graph rebuilds for a domain.
A domain-pack module that exposes a callable to the Lumina orchestrator as a named tool during a session.
A domain-pack library of pure functions available to systools adapters without LLM involvement.
The opaque, non-reversible identifier stored in the CTL in place of a real user identifier.
The upload → extract → SLM-interpret → DA-review → commit workflow for importing external content into a domain pack.
